designSystem.name: TaskFlow Design System (TFDS)
designSystem.description: A clean, productivity-focused design system built on principles of clarity, calm, and confidence. Emphasizes whitespace, clear hierarchy, and subtle animations that guide without distracting.

colorPalette.description: Productivity-focused color palette with clear semantic meaning for task states and priorities

primary.name: Primary Blue
primary.usage: Primary actions, active states, links, focus indicators

secondary.name: Focus Purple
secondary.usage: Focus Mode UI, AI-recommended tasks, priority scores

neutral.name: Neutral Gray Scale
neutral.usage: Backgrounds, borders, text hierarchy, disabled states

success.usage: Completed tasks, positive feedback, success states

error.usage: Overdue tasks, critical alerts, destructive actions

typography.description: Clean typography system using Inter for UI and JetBrains Mono for technical content

fontFamilies.name: Inter
fontFamilies.usage: Primary UI font for headings and body text

fontFamilies.name: JetBrains Mono
fontFamilies.usage: Task IDs, technical labels, code references

textStyles.name: Card Title
textStyles.usage: Task card and component card titles

components.name: TaskCard
components.description: The primary task display component. Shows task title, priority score badge, due date, project tag, and assignee avatar. Supports drag-and-drop, swipe actions, and expand/collapse for subtasks.

components.name: PriorityBadge
components.description: AI-computed priority score displayed as a circular badge (0-100). Color-coded from green (low urgency) through amber to red (critical). Includes a tooltip explaining the AI reasoning.

components.name: FocusModePanel
components.description: Full-screen overlay that shows only the top 3-5 AI-selected tasks with deep context. Features a timer, distraction blocker integration, and completion animations.

components.name: IntegrationHub
components.description: Dashboard showing all connected integrations with sync status indicators, last-sync timestamp, and quick-action buttons for each integration.

sizes.name: Default
sizes.usage: Standard icon size for navigation and actions

iconography.style: 2px stroke weight, open source with custom additions for AI-specific concepts

spacing.description: Consistent spacing system based on 8px base unit

designSystem.borderRadius: 6px for cards, 4px for buttons, 9999px for badges and avatars

userFlows.description: Guides new users through account setup, first project creation, integration connections, and AI calibration. Progressive disclosure pattern ensures users are not overwhelmed.
steps: Welcome screen with value proposition animation (3 seconds)
Account creation with SSO options (Google, Microsoft, GitHub)
Choose workspace type (Personal / Team / Enterprise)
Connect first integration (suggested: Google Calendar or Slack)
Create first project with AI-suggested template
Add 5 sample tasks or import from connected tool
AI calibration mini-quiz (5 questions about work style preferences)
Focus Mode tutorial with interactive walkthrough
Dashboard tour with contextual tooltips
successMetrics: Time to first Focus Mode session < 5 minutes

userFlows.description: The core daily workflow where users review AI-prioritized tasks, adjust priorities, and enter Focus Mode for deep work sessions.
steps: Open app to personalized dashboard with AI morning briefing
Review AI-prioritized task list with explanation tooltips
Optionally adjust task priorities via drag-and-drop or manual score override
Enter Focus Mode for top tasks
Complete tasks with one-click or swipe gesture
AI adapts priorities in real-time based on completions and time of day
End-of-day summary with productivity score and suggestions

userFlows.description: User connects external tools to TaskFlow Pro, configures sync rules, and maps external data to TaskFlow projects.
steps: Navigate to Settings > Integrations
Browse available integrations with categories and search
Select integration and authenticate via OAuth
Configure sync direction (one-way or bidirectional)
Map external projects/channels to TaskFlow projects
Set sync frequency and conflict resolution rules
Test connection with preview of synced data
Activate integration with success confirmation

wireframes.name: Dashboard - Default View
wireframes.description: Main dashboard layout with left sidebar navigation, center task list with AI priority scores, right panel showing task details and AI reasoning. Top bar includes search, notifications, and Focus Mode quick-launch button.
wireframes.notes: Responsive: sidebar collapses to icons at <1024px, task detail panel becomes slide-over at <768px

wireframes.name: Focus Mode - Active Session
wireframes.description: Full-screen Focus Mode with current task prominently displayed, minimal chrome, session timer, and subtle progress indicator. Next 2 tasks shown as small cards at bottom. Escape key or swipe-down to exit.
wireframes.notes: Dark mode strongly recommended for focus mode. Consider ambient sound integration.

wireframes.name: Integration Hub
wireframes.description: Grid of integration cards showing connected status, sync health, and quick actions. Unconnected integrations shown with 'Connect' CTA. Search and category filters at top.
wireframes.notes: Show most popular integrations first. Include 'Request Integration' link.

accessibilityRequirements.requirement: WCAG 2.2 Level AA compliance across all views
accessibilityRequirements.notes: All interactive elements must have visible focus indicators, minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio for text

accessibilityRequirements.requirement: Full keyboard navigation support including drag-and-drop alternatives
accessibilityRequirements.notes: Arrow keys for task reordering, Enter/Space for actions, Escape for dismissal

accessibilityRequirements.requirement: Screen reader compatibility with ARIA labels and live regions for dynamic updates
accessibilityRequirements.notes: AI priority changes must be announced via aria-live. Task completion feedback via aria-alert.

accessibilityRequirements.requirement: Reduced motion mode that disables animations and transitions
accessibilityRequirements.notes: Respect prefers-reduced-motion media query. Provide toggle in settings.

accessibilityRequirements.requirement: High contrast mode with increased border widths and inverted color scheme
accessibilityRequirements.notes: Windows High Contrast Mode support via forced-colors media query

usabilityTestingPlan.approach: Iterative testing with 5-8 participants per round, 3 rounds planned across design phases. Mix of moderated (remote) and unmoderated (UserTesting.com) sessions.

rounds.focus: Validate core task prioritization UX and Focus Mode concept
Test drag-and-drop, AI explanation tooltips, and integration setup flow
Screen reader testing, keyboard navigation, error states, and empty states

keyFindings: Users overwhelmingly prefer the 3-task Focus Mode over 5-task (Round 1)
AI priority explanations need to be shorter - users ignored paragraphs but engaged with 1-sentence summaries (Round 1)
Drag-and-drop for priority override felt natural but needs an undo mechanism (Round 2, preliminary)

designDecisions.decision: Use a fixed left sidebar instead of a top navigation bar
designDecisions.rationale: Task management benefits from vertical space for lists. Sidebar provides persistent navigation without sacrificing content area.

designDecisions.decision: Display AI priority as a 0-100 numeric score rather than High/Medium/Low labels
designDecisions.rationale: Numeric scores allow finer-grained prioritization and reduce ties. Users in research preferred granular control. Labels used for color coding only.

designDecisions.decision: Focus Mode enters full-screen overlay rather than a separate page
designDecisions.rationale: Overlay preserves context and allows quick exit. Separate page felt too disconnected in usability testing. Overlay approach aligns with distraction-reduction goals.
